title: pimelea pagophila
intro: Pimelea pagophila, also known as the frosty rice flower, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Australian Indigenous medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, or salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
